visionAnalysis.caption: PCB with visible defects including a broken trace and a short circuit.
visionAnalysis.defectDescription: There is a broken trace on the left side of the PCB, indicating an open circuit. Additionally, there is an unintended copper bridge between two traces on the right side, causing a short circuit.

normalizedDefect.description: There is a broken trace on the left side of the PCB, indicating an open circuit. Additionally, there is an unintended copper bridge between two traces on the right side, causing a short circuit.
normalizedDefect.reasoning: The open circuit could be due to improper etching or drilling, while the short circuit might be caused by a solder mask defect.

contributingFactors: Potential inconsistency in the etching solution concentration or temperature
Possible mechanical issue with the etching machine

actions.action: Inspect and calibrate the etching machine settings
Review and analyze the etching solution and its application process
Perform a thorough maintenance check on the etching machine

rootCause.reasoning: The defect report indicates an open circuit on the left side and a short circuit on the right side of the PCB. The high severity of these defects suggests a systemic issue rather than random occurrence. The defect density is low, which supports the idea that this is not a widespread issue but rather a specific problem in the etching process. The co-occurrence of open and short circuits is indicative of an etching problem, as these are often related to issues in the removal or deposition of copper. The AI-inferred equipment links point to the etching machine as a potential source of the defect.

checklist.requirement: No opens or shorts in conductors
checklist.evidence: There is a broken trace on the left side of the PCB, indicating an open circuit.

checklist.requirement: No shorts or opens in conductors
checklist.evidence: There is an unintended copper bridge between two traces on the right side, causing a short circuit.
